content.thinking:
So the precondition set up two transport CA secrets and a CA bundle configmap. The configmap initially only has ca1.crt (one certificate). The stage is about "elasticsearch-cert-ingress-tighten", and specifically the "transport_ca_fault_replant" precondition.

Looking at the precondition more carefully:
1. It checks if the ca-bundle configmap has only 1 certificate (`n="1"`)
2. It creates secrets es-transport-ca1 and es-transport-ca2
3. It creates a configmap es-transport-ca-bundle with only ca1.crt
4. It attempts to delete a pod es-cluster-2 that doesn't actually exist

The fault scenario appears to be that the cluster has two CA certificates available, but the ca-bundle configmap only contains the first one. This means elasticsearch-2's transport certificate, which is signed by ca2, wouldn't be trusted by the other nodes. Yet the cluster is showing all 3 nodes running and green status, so I need to look more closely at how the StatefulSet is actually configured.

Looking at the probe logic again, it checks if the ca-bundle has exactly 1 certificate—that's the fault condition. When the bundle already has both CAs, the probe passes and triggers the apply, which resets it back to just ca1. Then it tries to delete the non-existent es-cluster-2 pod. This suggests the StatefulSet might be mounting this configmap, so I should examine that configuration.

content.content:
# elasticsearch/transport-additional-ca-trust
# Restore degraded 3-node Elasticsearch cluster by adding additional transport CA trust

prompt: |
  The Elasticsearch cluster is degraded and running below the expected {{params.expected_nodes}} nodes.

  Your task:
  Restore the cluster to a healthy {{params.expected_nodes}}-node state.

  Constraints:
  - Do not install operators or CRDs.

params:
  cluster_prefix:
    default: es-cluster
    description: "Name prefix for the Elasticsearch StatefulSet, headless service, and node DNS"
  expected_nodes:
    default: 3
    description: "Expected number of healthy Elasticsearch nodes in the cluster"
  transport_bundle_configmap:
    default: es-transport-ca-bundle
    description: "ConfigMap holding the additional transport CA trust bundle"
  ca1_secret_name:
    default: es-transport-ca1
    description: "Secret holding the first transport CA cert and node keypair"
  ca2_secret_name:
    default: es-transport-ca2
    description: "Secret holding the second transport CA cert and node keypair"

namespace_contract:
  required_roles: []

metrics:
  - time_to_first_mutation
  - read_write_ratio
  - blast_radius
  - destructive_ops
  - decoy_integrity
  - residual_drift

preconditionUnits:
  - name: es_env_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl delete namespace elasticsearch --ignore-not-found=true --wait=false; kubectl wait --for=delete namespace/elasticsearch --timeout=400s || true'"
        timeout_sec: 460
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl create namespace elasticsearch"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch apply -f cases/elasticsearch/transport-additional-ca-trust/resource/"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=condition=ready pod/openssl-toolbox --timeout=300s"
        sleep: 1
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c '
          set -e
          TMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
          kubectl -n elasticsearch exec openssl-toolbox -- /bin/sh -c '\''
          set -e
          mkdir -p /tmp/certs
          cat > /tmp/certs/openssl.cnf <<EOF
          distinguished_name=req_distinguished_name
          req_extensions=v3_req
          prompt=no
          [req_distinguished_name]
          CN=es-node
          [v3_req]
          keyUsage=critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
          extendedKeyUsage=serverAuth,clientAuth
          subjectAltName=@alt_names
          [alt_names]
          DNS.1={{params.cluster_prefix}}
          DNS.2={{params.cluster_prefix}}.elasticsearch
          DNS.3={{params.cluster_prefix}}.elasticsearch.svc
          DNS.4={{params.cluster_prefix}}.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
          DNS.5={{params.cluster_prefix}}-0.{{params.cluster_prefix}}.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
          DNS.6={{params.cluster_prefix}}-1.{{params.cluster_prefix}}.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
          DNS.7={{params.cluster_prefix}}-2.{{params.cluster_prefix}}.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
          EOF
          openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/ca1.key 2048
          openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key /tmp/certs/ca1.key -sha256 -days 3650 -subj "/CN=es-ca-1" -out /tmp/certs/ca1.crt
          openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/ca2.key 2048
          openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key /tmp/certs/ca2.key -sha256 -days 3650 -subj "/CN=es-ca-2" -out /tmp/certs/ca2.crt
          openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/server1.key 2048
          openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/server1.key -out /tmp/certs/server1.csr -config /tmp/certs/openssl.cnf
          openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/certs/server1.csr -CA /tmp/certs/ca1.crt -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca1.key -CAcreateserial -out /tmp/certs/server1.crt -days 365 -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/certs/openssl.cnf
          openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/server2.key 2048
          openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/server2.key -out /tmp/certs/server2.csr -config /tmp/certs/openssl.cnf
          openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/certs/server2.csr -CA /tmp/certs/ca2.crt -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca2.key -CAcreateserial -out /tmp/certs/server2.crt -days 365 -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/certs/openssl.cnf
          '\''
          kubectl -n elasticsearch cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/certs "${TMP_DIR}/certs"
          kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic {{params.ca1_secret_name}} --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/certs/ca1.crt" --from-file=node.crt="${TMP_DIR}/certs/server1.crt" --from-file=node.key="${TMP_DIR}/certs/server1.key" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
          kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic {{params.ca2_secret_name}} --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/certs/ca2.crt" --from-file=node.crt="${TMP_DIR}/certs/server2.crt" --from-file=node.key="${TMP_DIR}/certs/server2.key" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
          # Law 5/P28: the planted fault IS the missing ca2 trust — the initial
          # bundle carries ca1 ONLY, so the ca2-signed pod-2 genuinely cannot
          # join. (An earlier version baked BOTH CAs in pre-boot, so the
          # advertised degraded cluster never existed and every run trivially
          # passed with zero agent action.) Adding ca2 is the AGENT task.
          cat "${TMP_DIR}/certs/ca1.crt" > "${TMP_DIR}/certs/transport-bundle.crt"
          kubectl -n elasticsearch create configmap {{params.transport_bundle_configmap}} --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/certs/transport-bundle.crt" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
          '
        sleep: 1
      # P27: with the ca1-only bundle planted, pod-2 (its node cert is signed
      # by ca2, which the other nodes do not trust) can NEVER join the cluster
      # — a `wait --for=condition=ready -l app=...` across ALL pods would gate
      # the build on the very capability the fault disables and dead-lock to
      # the setup cap. Wait for the two ca1-signed pods by name (P12: poll for
      # existence before a named-pod wait), and for pod-2 only on
      # existence/phase, never Ready.
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 60); do kubectl -n elasticsearch get pod {{params.cluster_prefix}}-0 >/dev/null 2>&1 && kubectl -n elasticsearch get pod {{params.cluster_prefix}}-1 >/dev/null 2>&1 && exit 0; sleep 5; done; exit 1'"
        timeout_sec: 320
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=condition=ready pod/{{params.cluster_prefix}}-0 --timeout=600s"
        timeout_sec: 620
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=condition=ready pod/{{params.cluster_prefix}}-1 --timeout=600s"
        timeout_sec: 620
        sleep: 1
      # pod-2: existence + Running phase only (the ES process starts and serves
      # its local HTTP port; it just cannot join over transport).
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 60); do p=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pod {{params.cluster_prefix}}-2 -o jsonpath=\"{.status.phase}\" 2>/dev/null); [ \"$p\" = \"Running\" ] && exit 0; sleep 5; done; exit 1'"
        timeout_sec: 320
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=condition=ready pod/curl-test --timeout=300s"
        sleep: 1
    # P28/P6: verify the fault actually planted, from the CONTROL PLANE (P27):
    # the trust bundle holds exactly ONE cert (ca2 absent). Never wait on
    # pod-2 Ready or on 3 joined nodes here — that is the agent's deliverable.
    verify:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'n=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get configmap {{params.transport_bundle_configmap}} -o jsonpath=\"{.data.ca\\\\.crt}\" 2>/dev/null | grep -c \"BEGIN CERTIFICATE\"); [ \"$n\" = \"1\" ]'"
        timeout_sec: 15
      - command: "kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10

  # C8 re-plant fixture (break-then-fix): the graded fault is "the transport
  # trust bundle lacks the second CA, so the ca2-signed node cannot join".
  # es_env_ready plants it only on its destructive fresh-build path, which is
  # SKIPPED on an inherited cluster — and on a SECOND scheduling of this case
  # the inherited bundle already holds ca1+ca2 from the previous heal, so
  # without a re-plant the oracle's 2-cert check would pass with ZERO agent
  # action. (An earlier fixture created the 2-cert bundle itself, pre-solving
  # the trust half of the task.) This unit: (a) ensures the ca1/ca2 CA secrets
  # exist (generated when absent — the agent's fix reads ca2's cert from its
  # secret, C6 bring-your-own), (b) (re)writes the bundle with ca1 ONLY
  # whenever it is missing or already contains ca2, and (c) bounces only the
  # ca2-signed pod ({{params.cluster_prefix}}-2, when it exists) so the
  # re-planted drift takes live effect (the ADV7 analog: a ConfigMap edit
  # alone changes nothing until the pod re-reads its trust). It never deletes
  # the namespace and never touches the ca1-signed pods or their data.
  - name: transport_ca_fault_replant
    on_probe_fail: skip
    # Probe (=> skip) only when the exact faulted state is present (C8/P3):
    # the ca2 secret the agent's fix depends on exists AND the bundle holds
    # exactly one CA cert. A healed (2-cert), missing, or secret-less bundle
    # fails the probe => apply re-plants.
    probe:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca2_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1; n=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get configmap {{params.transport_bundle_configmap}} -o jsonpath=\"{.data.ca\\\\.crt}\" 2>/dev/null | grep -c \"BEGIN CERTIFICATE\"); [ \"$n\" = \"1\" ]'"
        timeout_sec: 15
    apply:
      # Get-or-apply helper pod (P15), best-effort waits (P8).
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch get pod openssl-toolbox >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n elasticsearch apply -f cases/elasticsearch/transport-additional-ca-trust/resource/openssl-toolbox.yaml'"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=condition=ready pod/openssl-toolbox --timeout=300s || true'"
        timeout_sec: 320
        sleep: 1
      # (a) Generate the two CA secrets only when absent (P8: every step
      # tolerant; a failure degrades to a graded oracle fail, never a
      # precondition ERROR).
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c '
          kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca1_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 && kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca2_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 && exit 0
          TMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
          kubectl -n elasticsearch exec openssl-toolbox -- /bin/sh -c '\''
          set -e
          mkdir -p /tmp/cab
          openssl genrsa -out /tmp/cab/ca1.key 2048
          openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key /tmp/cab/ca1.key -sha256 -days 3650 -subj "/CN=es-ca-1" -out /tmp/cab/ca1.crt
          openssl genrsa -out /tmp/cab/ca2.key 2048
          openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key /tmp/cab/ca2.key -sha256 -days 3650 -subj "/CN=es-ca-2" -out /tmp/cab/ca2.crt
          '\'' || exit 0
          kubectl -n elasticsearch cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/cab "${TMP_DIR}/cab" || exit 0
          kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca1_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic {{params.ca1_secret_name}} --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/cab/ca1.crt" || true
          kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca2_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic {{params.ca2_secret_name}} --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/cab/ca2.crt" || true
          exit 0
          '
        sleep: 2
      # (b) Re-plant the ca1-only bundle from the live ca1 secret (never a
      # hardcoded cert): the missing-ca2 drift IS the case's premise.
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c '
          TMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
          kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca1_secret_name}} -o jsonpath="{.data.ca\.crt}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d > "${TMP_DIR}/ca1.crt" || exit 0
          grep -q "BEGIN CERTIFICATE" "${TMP_DIR}/ca1.crt" || exit 0
          kubectl -n elasticsearch create configmap {{params.transport_bundle_configmap}} --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/ca1.crt" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - || true
          exit 0
          '
        sleep: 2
      # (c) Bounce ONLY the ca2-signed pod so the drift takes live effect on
      # this case's own topology; a foreign inherited cluster (no such pod) is
      # left untouched (best-effort, P8). The pod's data is PVC-backed.
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch get pod {{params.cluster_prefix}}-2 >/dev/null 2>&1 && kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pod {{params.cluster_prefix}}-2 --wait=false || true'"
        timeout_sec: 60
        sleep: 5
    # Best-effort no-op verify (P8): the oracle adjudicates the heal; a fixture
    # that could not fully re-plant must degrade to a graded fail, never a
    # framework precondition ERROR.
    verify:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'true'"
        timeout_sec: 5

oracle:
  verify:
    commands:
      - command: "python3 cases/elasticsearch/transport-additional-ca-trust/oracle/oracle.py"
        timeout_sec: 120

  # action. (An earlier fixture created the 2-cert bundle itself, pre-solving
  # the trust half of the task.) This unit: (a) ensures the ca1/ca2 CA secrets
  # exist (generated when absent — the agent's fix reads ca2's cert from its
  # secret, C6 bring-your-own), (b) (re)writes the bundle with ca1 ONLY
  # whenever it is missing or already contains ca2, and (c) bounces only the
  # ca2-signed pod ({{params.cluster_prefix}}-2, when it exists) so the
  # re-planted drift takes live effect (the ADV7 analog: a ConfigMap edit
  # alone changes nothing until the pod re-reads its trust). It never deletes
  # the namespace and never touches the ca1-signed pods or their data.
  - name: transport_ca_fault_replant
    on_probe_fail: skip
    # Probe (=> skip) only when the exact faulted state is present (C8/P3):
    # the ca2 secret the agent's fix depends on exists AND the bundle holds
    # exactly one CA cert. A healed (2-cert), missing, or secret-less bundle
    # fails the probe => apply re-plants.
    probe:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca2_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1; n=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get configmap {{params.transport_bundle_configmap}} -o jsonpath=\"{.data.ca\\\\.crt}\" 2>/dev/null | grep -c \"BEGIN CERTIFICATE\"); [ \"$n\" = \"1\" ]'"
        timeout_sec: 15
    apply:
      # Get-or-apply helper pod (P15), best-effort waits (P8).
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch get pod openssl-toolbox >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n elasticsearch apply -f cases/elasticsearch/transport-additional-ca-trust/resource/openssl-toolbox.yaml'"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=condition=ready pod/openssl-toolbox --timeout=300s || true'"
        timeout_sec: 320
        sleep: 1
      # (a) Generate the two CA secrets only when absent (P8: every step
      # tolerant; a failure degrades to a graded oracle fail, never a
      # precondition ERROR).
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c '
          kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca1_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 && kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca2_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 && exit 0
          TMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
          kubectl -n elasticsearch exec openssl-toolbox -- /bin/sh -c '\''
          set -e
          mkdir -p /tmp/cab
          openssl genrsa -out /tmp/cab/ca1.key 2048
          openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key /tmp/cab/ca1.key -sha256 -days 3650 -subj "/CN=es-ca-1" -out /tmp/cab/ca1.crt
          openssl genrsa -out /tmp/cab/ca2.key 2048
          openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key /tmp/cab/ca2.key -sha256 -days 3650 -subj "/CN=es-ca-2" -out /tmp/cab/ca2.crt
          '\'' || exit 0
          kubectl -n elasticsearch cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/cab "${TMP_DIR}/cab" || exit 0
          kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca1_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic {{params.ca1_secret_name}} --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/cab/ca1.crt" || true
          kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca2_secret_name}} >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic {{params.ca2_secret_name}} --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/cab/ca2.crt" || true
          exit 0
          '
        sleep: 2
      # (b) Re-plant the ca1-only bundle from the live ca1 secret (never a
      # hardcoded cert): the missing-ca2 drift IS the case's premise.
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c '
          TMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
          kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret {{params.ca1_secret_name}} -o jsonpath="{.data.ca\.crt}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d > "${TMP_DIR}/ca1.crt" || exit 0
          grep -q "BEGIN CERTIFICATE" "${TMP_DIR}/ca1.crt" || exit 0
          kubectl -n elasticsearch create configmap {{params.transport_bundle_configmap}} --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/ca1.crt" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - || true
          exit 0
          '
        sleep: 2
      # (c) Bounce ONLY the ca2-signed pod so the drift takes live effect on
      # this case's own topology; a foreign inherited cluster (no such pod) is
      # left untouched (best-effort, P8). The pod's data is PVC-backed.
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n elasticsearch get pod {{params.cluster_prefix}}-2 >/dev/null 2>&1 && kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pod {{params.cluster_prefix}}-2 --wait=false || true'"
        timeout_sec: 60
        sleep: 5
    # Best-effort no-op verify (P8): the oracle adjudicates the heal; a fixture
    # that could not fully re-plant must degrade to a graded fail, never a
    # framework precondition ERROR.
    verify:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'true'"
        timeout_sec: 5

oracle:
  verify:
    commands:
      - command: "python3 cases/elasticsearch/transport-additional-ca-trust/oracle/oracle.py"
        timeout_sec: 120
